commit.message:
feat(themes): editable title bar, Roman numeral clone names, undo/delete in actions

Theme Studio title bar now shows the editable theme name (via new
titleContent slot on OverlayScaffold/OverlayTitleBar) instead of a
static label. Undo and delete icons moved to the title bar actions
slot — delete is always visible but disabled for new themes.

Cloning a built-in theme appends a Roman numeral to the display name
(e.g. "Aurora" → "Aurora II", "Aurora II" → "Aurora III"), skipping
collisions with existing theme names.

Removed Palette/Add page navigation icons from both ThemeSelector
and ThemeStudio title bars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

V3 Roadmap: 25 detailed phase documents (Phases 26-50)

Add comprehensive implementation specs for the next 25 development
phases, totaling 44,070 lines across 9 thematic blocks:

- Production Readiness (26-28): Backend integration, test suite, performance
- Visual & Feel (29-31): Character animation, mining juice, gacha polish
- Content Factory (32-34): Fact scaling, biome visuals, pixel art per fact
- Gameplay Expansion (35-37): Mine mechanics, combat, advanced pets
- Platform & International (38-40): iOS launch, web platform, i18n
- Growth & Analytics (41-43): Experiments, viral growth, co-op dives
- Education (44-46): Teacher dashboard, kid mode, learning research
- Endgame & Mastery (47-49): Achievement gallery, prestige, mine generation
- Platform Maturity (50): Open content ecosystem

Each document contains: overview, numbered sub-phases with TypeScript
code snippets, acceptance criteria, Playwright test scripts, verification
gates, and files-affected tables.

sccache-warmup: add missing commit_sha to failure notification (#25701)

## Summary
- The `nightly-build-failure-notify` workflow in sui-operations requires
`commit_sha` in the dispatch payload, but `sccache-warmup.yml` was not
sending it
- This caused every sccache warmup failure notification to fail
validation (`Missing required field: commit_sha`), silently dropping the
Slack alert
- Example failure:
https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui-operations/actions/runs/22673386881
- The `nightly.yml` workflow already sends `commit_sha` correctly — this
aligns `sccache-warmup.yml` with the same pattern

## Test plan
- [x] Verified `nightly.yml` sends `commit_sha` (existing working
reference)
- [x] Verified the receiver (`nightly-build-failure-notify.yml`)
validates `commit_sha` is non-empty
- [x] One-line addition of `"commit_sha": "${{ github.sha }}"` to the
dispatch payload

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Plan: Brand-Driven Editor Theme Generation (#193)

* docs: add plan for brand-driven editor theme generation

Design plan for extending agentkit-forge template generation to produce
VS Code workspace color customizations from a structured brand guide spec.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* docs: enrich editor theme plan with full brand spec schema

- Add complete brand.yaml schema with identity, colors (simple + detailed
  object format), typography, spacing, layout, motion, accessibility
- Include concrete AgentKit Forge brand spec with attribute-driven palette
  rationale (empowering→blue, collaborative→coral, inviting→teal, etc.)
- Include ChaufHER brand spec example for cross-brand validation
- Add color resolution rules (string vs {hex, role, rationale} object)
- Add brand validation table (required fields, hex format, contrast checks)
- Add brand-resolver.mjs as new dedicated module in file manifest
- Add _agentkit_theme sentinel key for managed section tracking

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* feat(brand): add brand-driven editor theme generation

Adds a complete brand spec system and editor theme generation pipeline
that produces VS Code/Cursor/Windsurf color customizations from
structured brand tokens.

New files:
- spec/brand.yaml: AgentKit Forge brand identity, colors (with rationale),
  typography, spacing, layout, motion, accessibility
- spec/editor-theme.yaml: Brand→editor color slot mapping with light/dark/both
  modes and multi-target outputs (vscode, cursor, windsurf)
- engines/node/src/brand-resolver.mjs: Color resolution (simple hex + detailed
  object), theme mapping resolver, brand validation, settings merge
- engines/node/src/__tests__/brand-resolver.test.mjs: 36 tests covering
  resolveColor, resolveBrandColor, resolveThemeMapping, validateBrandSpec,
  mergeThemeIntoSettings, isValidHex, getNestedValue
- overlays/__TEMPLATE__/brand.yaml: Per-repo brand override template
- overlays/__TEMPLATE__/editor-theme.yaml: Per-repo theme override with
  per-tool (cursor/windsurf) override support

Modified files:
- spec/project.yaml: Add hasBrandGuide, brandGuidePath, editorTheme section
- project-mapping.mjs: Add declarative mappings for brand/theme fields
- discover.mjs: Detect brand-guide and editor-theme artifacts
- init.mjs: Wire brand-guide/editor-theme into project scaffold
- synchronize.mjs: Add syncEditorTheme() with multi-output support,
  per-tool overlay merging, brand validation, and sentinel tracking

All 544 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* fix(brand): address code review issues for editor theme system

- Use explicit RESERVED_THEME_KEYS Set for per-tool overlay guard
- Preserve existing colorCustomizations in mergeThemeIntoSettings
- Delegate getNestedValue to canonical get() from project-mapping
- Support #RRGGBBAA (8-char) hex in isValidHex for VS Code alpha
- Move colorGuidance out of colors tree to avoid polluting color resolution
- Add integration tests for syncEditorTheme (6 tests)
- Add unit tests for alpha hex and merge-preserves behavior

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* fix(brand): address second review — mode handling, scaffold-once, security, docs

- Preserve separate light/dark mappings in 'both' mode instead of
  flattening into one object (light and dark resolved independently,
  dark wins on conflict in final merge)
- Add scaffold-once semantics: skip theme generation when
  .vscode/settings.json already exists unless --overwrite/--force
- Honor baseTheme (sets workbench.colorTheme) and fontFromBrand
  (sets editor.fontFamily from brand.typography.mono)
- Add path traversal protection on theme output paths — resolve
  and verify each outputPath stays inside tmpDir
- Fix overlay template: remove raw hex literal example, use brand
  token references only
- Update plan doc: correct syncEditorTheme signature, expand hex
  format rule to include #RRGGBBAA
- Add hasEditorThemeSource boolean mapping in project-mapping
- Add regression test for pre-existing settings.json merge
- Document 5 follow-up issues (brand HTML page, CSS tokens, brand
  inheritance, theme preview, WCAG contrast validation)

All 556 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* feat(brand): integrate brand system into agents, commands, and cross-platform templates

- Brand Guardian agent: add brand.yaml awareness (focus paths, responsibilities, conventions)
- Agent template: inject brandName, brandPrimaryColor, brandMono into template vars
- /project-review: add conditional brand.yaml validation in Phase 0.5
- /brand command: create new slash command with --validate, --palette, --theme, --contrast flags
- Cross-platform templates: add brand guide references to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md, GEMINI.md, WARP.md
- Marketing instructions: add brand-conditional styling guidance for Copilot

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* fix(brand): rewrite /brand command with proper frontmatter, $ARGUMENTS, and all invocation modes

- Add YAML frontmatter (description, allowed-tools) matching other commands
- Add $ARGUMENTS section with {{commandFlags}} table for flag documentation
- Add --init mode to scaffold brand.yaml interactively
- Add --generate mode to regenerate editor theme from specs
- Add --all flag to run full audit (validate + palette + theme + contrast)
- Make each mode a clearly separated section with explicit conditional triggers
- Expand allowed-tools to include Write, Edit, Bash for init/generate modes

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* docs(brand): fill documentation gaps in brand-guardian agent

- Add domain-rules (5 rules) separated from conventions per template pattern
- Add anti-patterns (4: hardcoding hex, manual settings.json edits, etc.)
- Add examples (3: simple hex entry, detailed object, theme mapping)
- Add Bash to preferred-tools for consistency with peer agents
- Expand accepts to include plan and investigate (was review-only)
- Add notifies: frontend (brand changes affect UI components)
- Add .cursor/settings.json and .windsurf/settings.json to focus paths
- Wire up domain-rules field in buildAgentVars (was hardcoded to empty)
- Add /brand command to COMMAND_GUIDE.md with flags, use cases, and
  decision tree entry

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

* fix(brand): address third review — per-output scaffold-once, template fixes

- synchronize.mjs: scaffold-once gate now checks per-output target instead
  of blocking all editor theme outputs when .vscode/settings.json exists
- brand.md: replace misleading `--only claude` with `--overwrite` in
  --generate mode; make lightness computation instructions explicit
- project-review.md: use {{brandGuidePath}} template var instead of
  hardcoded "brand.yaml"; handle both string and object color forms
- agents.yaml: clarify domain-rule that generated hex in settings.json is
  expected but manual edits are the anti-pattern
- commands.yaml: fix --validate default to false; clarify it runs implicitly
  when no other flags are given

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UmDYnuCaD2dJ3QDdZfxgJH

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: save mid-game state on Lock it in, not on Next Round

Players can see the answer overlay (target location, distance, points)
before tapping Next Round. If they refreshed between seeing the result
and advancing, they could replay that round with a different guess.

Moving saveMidGameState() into lockPin() — right after the server
confirms the score — means the save fires the instant the round is
locked. A refresh after that point will resume on the *next* round,
not re-open the current one.

Also removes the redundant saveMidGameState() call from the Next Round
handler since the save already happened at lock-in time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[P0] Swift/SwiftUI Native Menu Bar App Shell (#1)

* Add Swift/SwiftUI native menu bar app shell

- MeetingTranscriberApp/ Swift Package (no Xcode required, swift build)
- @main entry via NSApplication + AppDelegate (LSUIElement via setActivationPolicy)
- MenuBarController: NSStatusItem with bullet REC indicator (red/yellow/gray),
  contextual menu with Start Recording / Stop Recording / Quit
- ProcessManager: launches meeting-transcriber watch as subprocess with
  NO_REC_STATUS=1, parses stdout for recording state, auto-restarts on crash,
  writes start/stop commands to subprocess stdin
- AppState: shared state driving icon colour and menu item availability
- src/index.ts: support NO_REC_STATUS=1 env var -- skip rec-status subprocess
  and instead read start/stop commands from own stdin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Info.plist with LSUIElement=1 embedded into Swift binary

- Info.plist: LSUIElement=1 (menu-bar-only, no Dock icon), bundle ID,
  name, version, and NSMicrophoneUsageDescription
- Package.swift: embed plist into the __TEXT,__info_plist section via
  -sectcreate linker flags so macOS reads it from the standalone binary
  (no .app bundle required)

Completes the [P0] Swift/SwiftUI Native Menu Bar App Shell card. The
prior commit already delivered: MenuBarController (● REC indicator with
red/yellow/gray states, contextual menu), ProcessManager (launches
meeting-transcriber watch with NO_REC_STATUS=1, parses stdout for
state transitions, auto-restarts on crash, clean shutdown), AppState,
and src/index.ts stdin command reader for the NO_REC_STATUS path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Agent <agent@openclaw.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use HTTP status code 409 for already-archived detection (#482)

The string comparison on error message was not matching through React
Query's mutateAsync error chain. Now fetchJson attaches the HTTP status
code to thrown errors, and BulkArchiveModal checks status === 409 as
the primary match (with message string as fallback).

## 백엔드

- POST /admin/curation-themes/preview: 디바운스 미리보기 (count + top 3)

- DELETE /admin/curation-themes/:id: 커스텀 섹션 삭제 (preset 보호)

## 어드민 UI
- CreateThemeModal: 조건 빌더 모달 (카테고리/지역/태그 멀티셀렉트, 실시간 미리보기)

feat: auto-detect numbered/bulleted label:value lists as DataTables

Add detectLabelValueList() parser that finds 5+ consecutive "label: value"
lines (numbered, bulleted, dashed, or plain) and renders them as styled
DataTables while preserving surrounding prose text. Includes false-positive
guards (stopword check, value length limits) and smart column type detection
(currency/percentage/date). Currency values render in cyan, negatives in red.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add domain-based fallback filter in deep brand search

When only one of a brand's many Facebook pages appears in the initial
keyword scan, the merge in aggregate_by_advertiser() can't group the
other pages (they're invisible at that stage). The deep search then runs
domain-based queries (e.g. "glovbeauty.com") that return ads from all
12 pages, but the old page_name filter discards 11 of them.

Fix: in _deep_search_brand(), detect the brand's primary domain from
keyword_ads and use it as a secondary inclusion filter. Any ad whose
link_url resolves to the same root domain is accepted even if its
page_name wasn't known at scan time. Newly discovered page names are
added to page_names_set immediately so the page_id stage also covers them.

Also renames _extract_root_domain → extract_root_domain (with _ alias)
so it can be imported by market_pipeline.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Require draw.io diagrams during discovery and analysis - no ASCII art

Problem:
- Agents might create ASCII art when encountering or synthesizing visual content
- No explicit guidance on WHEN to create draw.io diagrams vs describing in text
- Discovery and analysis phases are where visualizations are most needed

Solution:
Updated CLAUDE.md and 5 key agent definitions to require draw.io diagrams:

1. **CLAUDE.md - Enhanced "No ASCII Art Policy"**
   - Added "When to create draw.io diagrams" section
   - Explicit triggers: discovery synthesis, analysis, design, "any time text alone unclear"
   - Common diagram needs: current state, dependencies, network flows, migrations, org structure
   - Reinforced validation enforcement

2. **discovery-synthesizer.md**
   - Added "Visualization During Discovery" section
   - Create diagrams when customer describes architecture/dependencies/workflows
   - Filename pattern: ##-current-state-{topic}.drawio
   - Common needs: network topology, app dependencies, datacenter layout, org structure

3. **infrastructure-analyst.md**
   - Added "Visualization" section to Rules
   - Create 01-current-state-architecture.drawio when topology needs visual representation
   - Cite data sources (RVTools, discovery notes) in diagram descriptions

4. **network-designer.md**
   - Added "Diagrams (REQUIRED)" section
   - ALWAYS create 04-network-architecture.drawio
   - Must show VPCs, subnets, TGW, connectivity, inspection points
   - Use official AWS icons (VPC, TGW, VPN, Direct Connect, Route 53)

5. **architecture-designer.md**
   - Added "Diagrams (REQUIRED)" section
   - ALWAYS create 02-target-architecture.drawio and 03-account-structure.drawio
   - Use official AWS service icons (EC2, RDS, S3, Lambda, etc.)

6. **application-analyst.md**
   - Added "Visualization" section
   - Create diagrams for complex application dependencies
   - Filename: 02-service-dependency-map.drawio

Key principles:
- "If a diagram would help, create one in draw.io"
- Discovery phase: capture customer's whiteboard/descriptions visually
- Analysis phase: visualize current state topology/dependencies
- Design phase: all architecture/network/security diagrams required
- NEVER use ASCII box-drawing characters - validation enforces this

Impact:
- Agents now know WHEN to create diagrams (not just HOW)
- Discovery synthesis captures visual customer explanations
- Analysis produces professional current-state diagrams
- Design phases guarantee architecture visualization
- Consistent diagram naming and AWS icon usage

fix(leonardo): use compact prefix instead of full VISUAL_DNA_BASE

VISUAL_DNA_BASE is 870 chars, leaving only ~620 chars for the scene
narrative within Leonardo's 1500-char limit. SD-based models also
prefer shorter style instructions — full scene narrative now fits.

fix+feat: poll option mutations, invite resilience, chat drag handle

Polls:

  without waiting for server roundtrip; then invalidates to sync
- useDeletePollOption: new hook with optimistic removal + rollback on error

  expired tokens

  server error message in Alert instead of generic wrapper text

  Business Account) instead of silently disabling

Chat channel (channelId.tsx):
- Drag handle: wrap in large handleArea (36px height, full width) for
  easier dismissal — matches pattern used in other modals
